calendar.matters:
Petition For Termination Of Further Proceedings And Discharge Of Administrator
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Continued To Dec-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia specially appearing for Attorney Sheila Robello.

Ms. Navia stated that she was informed by Ms. Robello that she has the document with the Client for signature and that the Client is in the process of sending it back and that a two week continuance would be appreciated. Status on close of estate is continued to December 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Navia is to inform Ms. Robello that if she is not in a position to close out the estate, she needs to file a petition for approval of a report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sheila Robello for Robert Gerson.

The Court stated that it was looking for the filing of the Inventory & Appraisal and the Petition for Final Distribution. Ms. Robello stated that she has the final drafts, that this is a zero asset estate, that she'll obtain the signatures, and that she had asked for a continuance of this matter too late. At the conclusion of Ms. Robello's comments, the Court continued the status on the filing of Inventory & Appraisal and status on the filing of the Petition for Final Distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration to November 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: M. Nuque. Matter not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Natalie Lupo for Robert Gerson.

The Court addressed the issue with regard to community property. Ms. Lupo stated that there was an issue with a specific separate account prior to the marriage. The Court stated that it was ready to make an order regarding 50% of the community property. Ms. Lupo stated that 50% would be acceptable in order to not cause any further delays. At the conclusion of Ms. Lupo's comments, the Court took the matter under submission and noted that Ms. Lupo was in agreement with item 2a in the Probate Examiner's notes.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Chan. Matter not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Continued To May-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Continued To Apr-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar Feb-19-2019 Continued To Mar-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar Dec-18-2018 Continued To Feb-19-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for Robert Gerson (Administrator).

The Court noted that there are two different dates of death in two different documents, questions whether this really is a creditor's claim, or not, stated that this seems to be figuring out what a Spouse's share is and that there are other procedures to do that. Ms. Robello stated that the Bank just needs a Court order, that they have a consent form from the Mother-in-Law, that the Father-in-Law's position is of non-opposition, spoke of a spousal property petition and stated that if the Court's wants an 850 Petition, she is requesting three months to do so and that the Decedent used the Husband's salary to fund the retirement account. The Court questions if he is a creditor, stated that it seems he has a viable claim to a share of the Decedent's estate as a Spouse and asks if this filing is appropriate. Ms. Robello requested a 60-day continuance. If Counsel concludes that this petition is appropriate, it will require filing of a memorandum of points and authorities. The Petition for Approval of Creditor's Claim is continued to February 19, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar Nov-06-2018 Continued To Nov-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Creditor S Claim Filed By Personal Representative
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Continued To Nov-06-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mercy Hall specially appearing for Sheila Robello.

The Court questions if this is an appropriate procedure, and stated that it doesn't appear to be a creditor's claim, and that it appears to be ownership and a right to an IRA. Ms. Hall stated that Ms. Robello is ill and is requesting a two week continuance. The Petition for Approval of Creditor's Claim is continued to November 6, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any supplemental filings are to be filed by October 24, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
